figures.caption: Figure 3: Ablation results for reasoning depth nlatent_steps on CIFAR-10.
Figure 2: Ablation results for supervision depth Nsupervision on CIFAR-10.

Table 1: Top-1 test accuracy (%) on CIFAR-10 across batch sizes. ViTRM achieves competitive accuracy with ∼3.6M parameters-4.7× fewer than ViT-Small and 23× fewer than ViT-Base. Best result per column in bold; best ViTRM result underlined.
Table 2: Top-1 test accuracy (%) on CIFAR-100 across batch sizes. ViTRM maintains strong performance on the more challenging 100-class task while using a fraction of baseline parameters.

Figure 1: ViTRM: Recursive Reasoning with Working Memory and Deep Supervision. Top: At each reasoning step t, the model alternates between two phases sharing the same transformer weights θ. In the Refine Memory phase, the concatenation of visual tokens x, answer token y, and memory tokens z is processed by the shared transformer T θ for M iterations, retaining only the updated memory z. In the Update Answer phase, the concatenation of y and z is fed to T θ to produce a new answer token y. This process is repeated T times and then passed to two shallow MLP heads: a classification head producing class logits ŷt, and a halting head producing a halting probability qt. Inference stops when qt > τ . Bottom: During training, deep supervision is applied at each of the N reasoning steps. At step n, the predicted output ŷn is supervised with a combined loss Ln = Lcls + Lhalt, using stop-gradient to prevent interference between steps.
